mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces equity markets to price in recessionary earnings cuts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounding above the 95 index level during recovery phases signals rapid capacity utilization improvements, which compresses risk premiums and drives short-term positive returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative in more than 58 percent of triggered observations, or rolling Sharpe ratio of the signal falls below zero over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate profit margins and trigger anticipated monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 60 percent of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2 percent annually signals robust economic expansion and strong corporate earnings momentum, which supports higher equity valuations over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of high-growth quarters, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 2 percent during tightening cycles increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, resulting in negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 58 percent of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 45 percent in out-of-sample testing.
